messages.content: Always use code blocks with the appropriate language tags. If asked for a table always format it using Markdown syntax.

In CMake, the `-G` switch is used to specify a generator, which is the build system to be used when generating the build files. When running CMake, you can specify the generator you want to use by providing the `-G` switch followed by the name of the generator.

For example, to generate build files for a Visual Studio project, you can use the following command:

```bash
cmake -G "Visual Studio 16 2019" path_to_source
```

Here, `-G "Visual Studio 16 2019"` tells CMake to use the Visual Studio 2019 generator to create the necessary files for building the project.

Different generators are available depending on your platform and installed tools, such as makefiles, Ninja, Visual Studio, Xcode, etc. Each generator has its own set of options and capabilities for building projects.

You can run `cmake --help` to see the list of available generators on your system.
what is 16 in visual studio name

In the Visual Studio generator name, the "16" represents the version number of Visual Studio. In this case, "16" corresponds to Visual Studio 2019. 

Visual Studio versions are commonly referred to by their year of release, such as:

- Visual Studio 2010
- Visual Studio 2012
- Visual Studio 2013
- Visual Studio 2015
- Visual Studio 2017
- Visual Studio 2019

So when using the `-G "Visual Studio 16 2019"` generator in CMake, you are specifying that you want to generate build files compatible with Visual Studio 2019.
